claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess this properly, I'll need to gather some information about your circumstances and your children.

Let me start with a basic question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I have one child I'd like to ask about - my son Alex. He's 17 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Before I can assess Alex's eligibility, I need to understand your family situation better.

**Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you. I need to gather a bit more information about Alex's circumstances.

**Is Alex currently in full-time education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in education at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Let me check the eligibility rules to ensure I have the correct criteria.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the eligibility rules I've reviewed, I can see that since Alex is 17 years old and not in approved education or training, there may be a limited window for eligibility.

**I need to know: When did Alex leave education or training? Specifically, has it been within the last 20 weeks?**

This is important because there's a transitional provision that allows 20 weeks of Child Benefit for 16 or 17 year olds who leave education or training if they register with certain services.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's been longer than that. He left school quite a while ago now.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. I have one more important question:

**Is Alex currently working? If so, is he working 24 hours or more per week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't working at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Let me make sure I have all the details correct before I provide my assessment:

- Alex is 17 years old
- He lives with you
- He is not in approved education or training
- He left education/training longer than 20 weeks ago
- He is not currently working

Based on the eligibility rules for Child Benefit, I can now provide you with my assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Outcome

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

Alex is 17 years old and not in approved education or training. While Child Benefit can be extended for 16-17 year olds in education or training, there is only a limited 20-week transitional period after they leave education if they register with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. Since Alex left education longer than 20 weeks ago, this transitional period has expired, and he no longer qualifies for Child Benefit.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change, feel free to reach out.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. There is a transitional provision that allows 20 weeks of Child Benefit if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. However, Alex left education longer than 20 weeks ago, so this transitional period has expired. Therefore, Alex is no longer eligible for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit because he is 17 years old, not in approved education or training, and the 20-week transitional period following his departure from education has ended. Child Benefit for 16-17 year olds is only payable if they remain in approved education or training, or if they have recently left and are registered with specified services (within 20 weeks)."

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 17 years old and no longer in approved education or training. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. There is a transitional provision that allows 20 weeks of Child Benefit if a 16 or 17 year old leaves education or training and registers with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed services. However, Alex left education longer than 20 weeks ago, so this transitional period has expired. Therefore, Alex is no longer eligible for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: Alex does not qualify for Child Benefit because he is 17 years old, not in approved education or training, and the 20-week transitional period following his departure from education has ended. Child Benefit for 16-17 year olds is only payable if they remain in approved education or training, or if they have recently left and are registered with specified services (within 20 weeks)."
